policy_text: Case studies must be anonymized and use 'Anonymous Identifier' for customer names unless explicitly authorized in writing.
Case studies are intended for Bid Managers, Sales Directors, and Proposal Strategy Consultants to address 'Past Performance' or 'Relevant Experience' RFP thresholds.
The Case Studies Library applies exclusively to answering 'Past Performance' or 'Relevant Experience' RFP thresholds.
The logistics transformation project 'Project E-Com' must achieve 99.95% active availability SLA universally.
The logistics transformation project 'Project E-Com' must reduce WAN configuration updates from 7 days to 4 hours via orchestration.
The logistics transformation project 'Project E-Com' must achieve a 30% reduction in aggregate WAN Net TCO versus legacy MPLS.
The logistics transformation project 'Project E-Com' must deploy a converged Managed SD-WAN and NGFW architecture.
The logistics transformation project 'Project E-Com' must use Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) to activate 50 hubs per month.
The private banking project 'Project Core-Sync' must deploy an Active/Active HA Edge matrix across 500 branches.
The private banking project 'Project Core-Sync' must achieve sub-8ms latency between NESCO data hub and Azure Central India.
The private banking project 'Project Core-Sync' must ensure 100% telemetry DPDP Act compliance with zero outbound internet egress points on branch LANs.
The private banking project 'Project Core-Sync' must anchor to a secure Azure ExpressRoute core for banking database hosting.
The private banking project 'Project Core-Sync' must implement stateful <50ms application-aware routing prioritizing core banking traffic.
The company has the capability to deploy 100,000+ M2M SIMs securely bound to a localized Custom APN (cAPN) for secure telemetry ingestion.
Project E-Com is governed by the Enterprise Sales Director (West) for Multi-Crore TCV Executed 2023.
Project Core-Sync is governed by the Head of Managed Services for SLA Penalty Audits (0% Payouts).
Modifying hard metrics (e.g., claiming 1,000 sites instead of 350) in a bid response is legally prohibited and constitutes fraudulent misrepresentation.
This Company Fact Sheet provides verified, highly structured corporate information for VI Business (India) and is designed as the internal source-of-truth for bid coordinators and account managers.
Use the Company Fact Sheet for all quick-reference organizational facts in RFP 'Bidder Information' sections.
Do not alter the financial or structural facts below without explicit Deal Desk variance approval.
Tier-1 Hub requires 99.99% network availability.
Metrics provided represent aggregate enterprise scale capabilities.
The company's registered office is located at Suman Tower, Plot No. 18, Sector 11, Gandhinagar - 382011, Gujarat.
The company's corporate headquarters is located at Birla Centurion, Century Mills Compound, Worli, Mumbai - 400030, Maharashtra.
Primary NOC/SOC locations are in Pune, Maharashtra, with secondary/disaster recovery (DR) locations in Mumbai, Maharashtra.
Tier-1 Availability SLA is 99.99% as part of the VI Business Standard Commitment.
Tier-2/3 Availability SLA is 99.95% as part of the VI Business Standard Commitment.
SD-WAN Failover Target must be less than 50ms.
Data Residency is 100% India-localized and certified under the DPDP Act 2023.
Priority 1 (P1) Security Incidents require 15-minute acknowledgment and <4 hours for containment.
The company is designated as an Azure ExpressRoute Partner and AWS Direct Connect Partner.
Terrestrial Reach includes 250,000+ km of domestic optical fiber route coverage.
Wireless Edge Reach covers 98%+ population across all 28 states and 8 Union Territories.
Data Center Footprint includes 12 major Tier-III facilities nationwide, with primary hyperscaler hubs in Mumbai (NESCO) and Chennai.
Managed Network Services include co-managed and fully managed SD-WAN overlays with Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) for N-site deployments.
Cloud Interconnects provide carrier-grade private peering via Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute and AWS Direct Connect.
Managed Security (SOC) includes ITIL v4 compliant security monitoring from localized Indian Tier-III SOC facilities.
Enterprise IoT offers a unified EMM platform for extreme-scale SIM/eSIM deployments via localized Custom APNs (cAPN).
The company holds ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification for Information Security Management.
The company holds ISO 20000-1:2018 certification for IT Service Management.
The company is SOC 2 Type II compliant, with detailed control clauses in the Compliance and Certifications Pack [VIB-COMP-003].
Annual Revenue (Entity Level) exceeds INR 40,000 Crore.
Active IoT Connections exceed 2.5 million via the company's proprietary mobility platform.
Tier-1 Availability SLA is 99.99% as part of the VI Business Standard Commitment.
Tier-2/3 Availability SLA is 99.95% as part of the VI Business Standard Commitment.
SD-WAN Failover Target must be less than 50ms.
Data residency must be 100% India-localized and certified under the DPDP Act 2023.
Priority 1 (P1) security incidents must be acknowledged within 15 minutes.
Priority 1 (P1) security incidents must be contained within 4 hours.
The company is designated as an Azure ExpressRoute Partner and AWS Direct Connect Partner.
Strict compliance with India's Department of Telecommunications (DoT) Unified License (UL) mandates is required.
Routing and traffic handling must enforce lawful interception as mandated by Indian telecom security rules.
ISO 27001/SOC 2 certifications must be stored in SharePoint/VIB_Compliance_Vault/Certs and provided upon NDA execution.
AWS/Azure Partner status must be publicly verifiable via public.aws.amazon.com/partners.
Financial figures must represent VIL consolidated results without breaking out specific business unit revenues.
Commitments beyond stated SLAs (e.g., 99.999% uptime or <2 hour P1 resolution) require explicit Solution Engineering and Deal Desk pricing reviews.
Submitting expired certification evidence to an enterprise procurement board constitutes a lethal disqualification risk.
Vodafone Idea Limited operates under a full Unified License granted by the Government of India (DoT).
Operations strictly obey DoT security edicts for lawful interception compliance.
VI Business maintains mandatory intercept gateways capability on core routing junctions to cooperate with Indian law enforcement mandates.
Core access bandwidth adheres to TRAI Quality of Service (QoS) regulations for network uptime, contention limits, and latency boundaries.
VI Business functions as a Data Processor respecting enterprise payload traversing managed SD-WANs and ExpressRoutes.
100% of network telemetry, identity token handshakes, ITIL support tickets, and SIEM security logs are physically retained within Pune/Mumbai/Chennai data centers.
No telemetry data or raw network traffic crosses international sovereign boundaries without explicit written consent from the Customer and adherence to DoT/DPDP cross-border frameworks.
VI Business maintains independent third-party audit recertifications across global security standards (ISO, SOC 2).

policy_text: All data center and security references align strictly with DPDP Act 2023 localization requirements.
Do not overstate capabilities outside these bounds.
This Corporate Profile serves as the standard organizational definition for VI Business (India).
The Enterprise Business Unit operates as a distinct division within Vodafone Idea Limited, governed by dedicated leadership to ensure B2B focus.
The VP - IoT & Mobility directs EMM platforms and scales the 2.5 million+ SIM base.
The VP - Core Networks & Cloud manages the architectural lifecycle of SDN and Cloud Interconnects.
The Corporate Profile is intended for use by Bid managers, Solution Architects, and Account Directors.
The Corporate Profile must be used as the source material for RFP sections requesting corporate overviews, strategy alignment, and operational footprints.
Deliver secure "Cloud-to-Edge" networking by converging Managed SD-WAN, hyperscaler interconnects, and ITIL v4 Managed SOC into a unified, SLA-backed managed service.
Provide identical, carrier-grade service architectures across multiple highly-regulated sectors, including Retail, Banking & Finance (BFSI), Manufacturing, IT/ITES, and Logistics.
Support extensive Phased Migration Frameworks capable of scaling to hundreds of sites per quarter.
Operate customized routing policies that prioritize business-critical payloads (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, core banking systems) with <50ms failover.
Network Reach spans all 28 states and 8 Union Territories, integrated into massive domestic fiber backhauls.
Data Centers include 12 major Tier-III facilities nationwide, with primary hyperscaler/cloud on-ramps anchored in Mumbai (NESCO) and Chennai.
NOC/SOC Operations have primary monitoring facilities in Pune, with secondary Disaster Recovery (DR) fallback in Mumbai.

VI Business provides end-to-end service consolidation integrating overlay (SD-WAN), underlay (LTE/Broadband), boundary security (Managed SOC), and endpoint visibility (IoT EMM) under a single, accountable MSA with rigid SLA penalties.
VI Business is fully compliant with all Department of Telecommunications (DoT) unified licensing and TRAI QoS norms.
VI Business ensures 100% data localization for SOC SIEM logs and network analytics under the DPDP Act, 2023.
Cross-border data transfer for SOC SIEM logs and network analytics is prohibited without explicit, documented corporate authorization from the Data Fiduciary.
The 'IoT Scale' claim is evidenced by the Internal HLR/HSS Audit Report, owned by the VP, IoT Operations.
The 'Network SLA Validation' claim is evidenced by the Monthly Service Delivery Analytics Dashboard, owned by the Head of Service Assurance.
VI Business does not manage end-user compute devices (laptops/desktops) unless explicitly scoped in a Mobile Device Management (MDM) Statement of Work (SOW).
Service Coverage and Infrastructure Footprint Document [VIB-TECH-004] must be referenced for precise fiber and cell site constraints.
The Customer Reference Matrix is strictly for internal use when a Customer explicitly demands 'Contactable Client References' or 'Reference Call Scheduling'.
Bid Managers must not arbitrarily append reference contacts to a bid without explicitly verifying the 'Last Verified' validation date.
Stale contacts with expired 'Last Verified' dates must not be used, as they may negatively impact RFP scoring.
Customer references must maintain legally signed consent forms permitting oral or email-based operational validation checks.
The company provides a 350+ Node SD-WAN & Unified Managed Security (SOC) Transformation solution with Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) and cloud SIEM ingestion capabilities.
The company ensures SLA adherence for the 350+ Node SD-WAN & SOC Transformation solution.
Customer references for the 350+ Node SD-WAN solution are authorized for direct email verification and 30-minute structured reference calls.
The 350+ Node SD-WAN solution's 'Last Verified / Re-certified Date' must not exceed 2024-01-15.
The company provides a 500+ Branch Network solution with Active-Active HA Edge and Data Localization compliant with the DPDP Act.
The 500+ Branch Network solution guarantees <50ms stateful failover across terrestrial and LTE links.
The 500+ Branch Network solution ensures Azure ExpressRoute stability.
Phone calls for the 500+ Branch Network reference require a two-week lead time warning.
The 500+ Branch Network solution's 'Last Verified / Re-certified Date' must not exceed 2024-02-28.

policy_text: The owner of the Customer Reference Matrix is the VP, Customer Success (Enterprise).
Legal Counsel and Enterprise Sales Heads must review the Customer Reference Matrix.
The Customer Reference Matrix must be reviewed quarterly.
The matrix must be purged of personnel who have transitioned corporations or explicitly revoked consent.
Bid Managers must email the designated Internal Account Director prior to proposing a reference in a live bid if the customer is experiencing a severe P1 operational escalation.

The Detailed Solution Datasheets Library provides the definitive engineering boundary for solution proposals by specifying rigorous, exact metric constraints of VI Business (India)’s deployed architectures.
The library is intended for verbatim insertion into RFP 'Technical Specification' annexures or 'Compliance to Specifications' columns requiring structured limits.
Max Validated IPSec Throughput for Baseline Edge (Branch) is constrained to 200 Mbps - 500 Mbps.
Max Validated IPSec Throughput for High-Performance Edge (Hub) is constrained to 2 Gbps - 5 Gbps.
Committing to throughputs or limits beyond the specified bounds breaches the underlying hardware OEM capacity matrix and voids MSA liability constraints.
The Detailed Solution Datasheets Library includes Edge Routing Specifications for Managed SD-WAN, including BGP/OSPF Routing Engine parameters.
Standard Hot Log Retention: 90 Days (Active searching immediately available)
Active searching is immediately available for hot logs.
Cold Storage Archival: 12 Months natively.
Threat Intelligence Automation: Hourly ingestion of STIX/TAXII threat intel feeds.
Data Localization: 100% Hosted locally within Pune/Mumbai Tier-III DCs.
ExpressRoute (Azure) uses Layer 3 eBGP Native for interconnects.
Direct Connect (AWS) uses Layer 2/3 (Transit Gateway via VIF) for interconnects.
ExpressRoute (Azure) Port Bandwidth Options: 1 Gbps to 10 Gbps.
Direct Connect (AWS) Port Bandwidth Options: 1 Gbps to 100 Gbps (DC dependent).
ExpressRoute (Azure) Availability SLA: 99.99% (Subject to dual ER circuits).
Direct Connect (AWS) Availability SLA: 99.9% base (Dual Ports).
ExpressRoute (Azure) Latency Boundary: <8ms (NESCO to Azure Central India).
Direct Connect (AWS) Latency Boundary: <8ms (NESCO to AWS ap-south-1).
Internal Hardware Verification is required for evidence and proof-artifact mapping.
ExpressRoute (Azure) has an Availability SLA of 99.99% when using dual ER circuits.
Direct Connect (AWS) has an Availability SLA of 99.9% base with dual ports.
ExpressRoute (Azure) latency boundary must be less than 8ms between NESCO and Azure Central India.
Direct Connect (AWS) latency boundary must be less than 8ms between NESCO and AWS ap-south-1.
High-end (10 Gbps+) firewall throughput requires disabling certain extreme deep-packet sandboxing routines to prevent Edge CPE processor maxing out and network degradation.
Solution Architects must design payload profiles correctly to avoid exceeding Edge CPE processor capacity and causing network degradation.
VI Business (India) commercial negotiators must use pre-approved legal fallback positions, risk ratings, and mandatory escalation paths for non-standard customer RFP terms.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Liability is capped at 100% of the total fees paid by the Customer for the specific affected Service during the twelve (12) months immediately preceding the claim event.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Liability is capped at 100% of the total annualized contract value (Annual TCV) for the specific year in which the claim arises.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: VI Business cannot accept unlimited liability or liability extending to indirect, punitive, or consequential damages (e.g., loss of profits/revenue).
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Exceptional approval for Limitation of Liability deviations requires CFO and General Counsel sign-off.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Maximum monthly SLA penalty is capped at 10% of the monthly recurring charge (MRC) for the specific impacted link/service.
source_section: 3.2 Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalties / Liquidated Damages
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: SLA penalties for Tier-1 Hub Sites requiring 99.99% availability may be increased to a soft cap of 15% of the MRC.
source_section: 3.2 Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalties / Liquidated Damages
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: SLA penalties exceeding 20% of the MRC or penalties deducting from unrelated services (e.g., penalizing Enterprise IoT billing for an SD-WAN failure) are prohibited.
source_section: 3.2 Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalties / Liquidated Damages
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Exceptions for Liability/Indemnities clauses require approval from the Regional CFO via the Commercial_Deviation_Form_v3 (Salesforce).
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Exceptions for Privacy (DPDP) clauses require approval from the Data Privacy Officer via the DPO_Exception_Ticket (ServiceNow).
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Termination for convenience is not permitted during the initial lock-in period (typically 36 months).
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Termination for convenience is permitted after 18 months, subject to a 90-day notice period and mandatory full repayment of unrecovered capital/installation costs.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Termination must not allow the customer to walk away without payment of stranded hardware values.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: VI Business reserves the unilateral right to intercept, monitor, and hand over data to Indian governmental authorities as mandated by the Unified License, without prior notice to the customer.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Lawful interception compliance is a regulatory mandate with no fallback wording permitted.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Liability/Indemnities exceptions require approval by the Regional CFO via the Commercial_Deviation_Form_v3 (Salesforce).
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Privacy (DPDP) exceptions require approval by the Data Privacy Officer via the DPO_Exception_Ticket (ServiceNow).
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: The General Counsel, Enterprise Legal is the owner of the governance and ownership framework.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Deal Desk and Corporate Finance are required to review governance and ownership policies.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Governance and ownership policies must be reviewed annually.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: Fallbacks can only be initiated during formalized BAFO or MSA negotiation stages, not in initial RFP draft compliance grids.
source_filename: Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide.pdf

policy_text: If a customer RFP requests 'Unlimited Liability,' it must be marked 'Partially Compliant' or 'Non-Compliant,' with the 'Fallback Wording' from Section 3.1 included in the deviation column.

policy_text: Compliance matrices must never mark 'Compliant' on red-line terms for 'Unlimited Liability' customer RFPs.

policy_text: Committing to customized deployment cadences outside the 5-Phase structure requires approval from the Enterprise PMO Director.
Phase 1 deliverables must include an Approved Low-Level Design (LLD) and Pilot Site Acceptance.
Phase 1 activities must include physical provisioning of primary Cloud Interconnects at Mumbai/Chennai data hubs and migration of 5-10 Pilot locations.
Phase 2 deliverables must include a Hub Site Acceptance Document and QoS Baseline Report.
Phase 2 activities must involve migration of Tier-1 Hub locations and successful BGP/OSPF peerings with customer cloud environments.
Phase 3 capabilities must include high-volume ZTP deployments across independent geographic zones, scaling to hundreds of sites per quarter.
Phase 3 deliverables must include a Wave A Site Acceptance Bundle.
Phase 4 capabilities must include migration of lower-tier branch sites with customized LTE deployments in fixed-line RoW-constrained regions.
Phase 4 deliverables must include Completed Site Deployments.
Phase 5 must include a 30-day Hypercare period for monitoring network stability before handing over to Service Assurance teams.
Phase 5 deliverables must include a Project Closure Document.
The Project Plan Master must be stored in SharePoint/VIB_Delivery/Templates/MSP and owned by the Enterprise PMO.
The Site Sign-off Matrix must be stored in SharePoint/VIB_Delivery/Gateways and owned by the Delivery Director.
Delivery managers must enforce 'Go/No-Go' gateways prior to initiating mass rollouts (Phase 3).
SIEM data logging and SOC event ingestion must be mathematically verified during the Pilot Phase before any further edge appliance drops.
The Project Plan Master artifact must be stored in the repository at 'SharePoint/VIB_Delivery/Templates/MSP' and owned by the Enterprise PMO.
The Site Sign-off Matrix artifact must be stored in the repository at 'SharePoint/VIB_Delivery/Gateways' and owned by the Delivery Director.
The Head of PMO, Enterprise Delivery is designated as the owner for governance decisions.
The VP Service Operations and Lead Enterprise Architect are designated as reviewers for governance decisions.
Governance reviews must occur annually.
Phase velocity assumptions must reflect local Right of Way (RoW) realities for fiber deployment in Tier-3 towns.
Project timelines formally commence only after the Customer structurally signs the baseline PID and LLD definitions.
The Industry Solutions Portfolio is intended for use by Sector Leads, Presales Consultants, and Strategic Account Directors.
The Industry Solutions Portfolio is applicable for Proposal sections requiring 'Domain Intelligence', 'Vertical Experience', or pre-packaged industry baseline responses.

In areas with inferior terrestrial fiber infrastructure, the solution must use ZTP-enabled SD-WAN edge nodes with dual Business LTE SIMs as the primary WAN until local Right of Way (RoW) clears for fiber/Broadband.
The solution must transition to Broadband/Fiber connectivity once local Right of Way (RoW) clears.
The company can converge fleet IoT tracking alongside physical warehouse networking natively over a unified management layer.
The solution must comply with RBI and SEBI regulatory oversight and zero-tolerance policies for Data Sovereignty.
source_section: 3.2 Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) Matrix

policy_text: The solution must ensure all transaction data and logs comply with the DPDP Act 2023 by siloing ingress/egress logs in Indian SOC facilities.
source_section: 3.2 Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) Matrix

policy_text: The solution must achieve <50ms failover for high-frequency core-banking transactions.
source_section: 3.2 Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) Matrix

policy_text: The solution must route traffic exclusively over private Layer 2 MPLS equivalents and avoid all public internet paths.
source_section: 3.2 Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) Matrix

policy_text: The solution must guarantee no transaction logs or DPI payloads cross international internet gateways.
source_section: 3.2 Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) Matrix

policy_text: The solution must meet the security stance demanded by financial auditors by natively preventing international data transfer.
source_section: 3.2 Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) Matrix

policy_text: Nationwide IoT SIM distributions must be completely isolated via dedicated Custom APN (cAPN) fabrics.
Local plant internet exposure must be protected via high-throughput NGFWs supporting 4Gbps+ throughput.
Critical IoT infrastructure must be air-gapped via cAPN routing to prevent external public IP scanning or ransomware pivots.
The Logistics Overlay must use the architectural diagram code 'Arch_Logistics_Standard.vsdx' and maintain a 99.95% SLA.
The Banking Overlay must use the architectural diagram code 'Arch_BFSI_ZeroTrust.vsdx' and comply with DPDP Act Localization Certification.
Vertical bundles must only package standard SKUs and cannot invent non-catalog SKUs for a single industry.
Creating non-catalog SKUs for a specific industry is prohibited without completing rigorous NPI (New Product Introduction) gating.
VI Business (India) supports bi-directional RESTful API integration for ITSM systems (ServiceNow & BMC Helix) to enable Incident Creation, Status Update, and Closure State Webhooks.
System-level e-Bonding ensures synchronous synchronization of Incident Creation, Status Update, and Closure State Webhooks between VI NOC/SOC and the customer environment.
Native log forwarding via Syslog/CEF formats to cloud-hosted SIEM targets is fully supported.
SIEM log forwarding mandates require the target repository to be hosted within the Republic of India under the DPDP Act 2023.

Bespoke integration development (custom middleware for proprietary customer application alarms) is prohibited under the standard Managed Services MSA and requires a separate Professional Services SOW.
VI Business SD-WAN orchestration portals, EMM IoT dashboards, and reporting portals support SAML 2.0 and OAuth 2.0 for Single Sign-On (SSO) with major IdPs.
Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) must securely map to the customer's internal AD security groups using Just-In-Time (JIT) provisioning.
Integration capability documentation is applicable for responding to RFP sections demanding 'System Interoperability,' 'e-Bonding,' and 'API Maturity.'
e-Bonding is defined as bidirectional ticket integration linking VI Business’s ITSM to the Customer’s ITSM via REST APIs.
SIEM is defined as a Security Information and Event Management platform.
The VI EMM platform exposes a comprehensive, OpenAPI 3.0 specification REST API.
API capabilities include endpoints for bulk SIM lifecycle actions (activation/suspension).
API capabilities include endpoints for customized data pooling allocation queries.
API capabilities include endpoints for hourly telemetry aggregation reports used for proprietary fleet tracking integrations.
All exposed VI Business integration endpoints are secured exclusively via TLS 1.3.
Strict token-based authentication is mandated for all integrations.
Permitted authentication flows include OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Grant for server-to-server e-Bonding.
Permitted authentication flows include Mutual TLS (mTLS) certificate pinning for high-security banking/financial connections.
Static API keys are explicitly deprecated for Tier-1 integrations.
The ITSM (ServiceNow) integration specification document is owned by the ITSM Automation Lead.
The EMM API Repository integration specification document is owned by the IoT Platform Manager.
The customer assumes absolute responsibility for configuring their side of the e-Bonding webhook receivers.
Endpoint latency exceeding 5000ms from the customer's ITSM receiver automatically suspends the VI Business Webhook retry queue.
VI Business (India) does not write or host application-layer code required on the customer’s side to consume OpenAPI endpoints.
Support SLAs defined in this handbook are contractually legally binding metrics. Modifications require formal legal review before RFP submission.
Support operations must run on a continuous 24/7/365 shift rotation spanning three shifts (0600-1400, 1400-2200, 2200-0600 IST).
source_section: 3.1 Support Footprint, Tiers, and Handoff Criteria

policy_text: Tier 1 (L1 Service Desk) must handle 100% of initial triage within <15 minutes of alarm generation.
source_section: 3.1 Support Footprint, Tiers, and Handoff Criteria

policy_text: Tier 1 (L1 Service Desk) must provide multilingual support in English, Hindi, and 6+ regional Indian languages.
source_section: 3.1 Support Footprint, Tiers, and Handoff Criteria

policy_text: Tier 2 (L2 NOC/SOC Analysts) must consist of certified engineers performing deep-packet inspection, active firewall mitigation, SD-WAN policy verification, and branch hardware isolation.
source_section: 3.1 Support Footprint, Tiers, and Handoff Criteria

policy_text: L1 to L2 handoff must occur if L1 cannot restore service remotely within 30 minutes, or instantly if a P1 security IOC is detected.
source_section: 3.1 Support Footprint, Tiers, and Handoff Criteria

policy_text: Tier 3 (L3 Subject Matter Experts/Architects) must handle advanced persistent threats (APTs) and complex BGP/OSPF routing anomalies traversing the core optical NLD.
source_section: 3.1 Support Footprint, Tiers, and Handoff Criteria

policy_text: Tier 3 (L3) must exclusively manage OEM vendor (e.g., Cisco TAC) interactions.
source_section: 3.1 Support Footprint, Tiers, and Handoff Criteria

policy_text: ITIL v4 Incident Management must align with the VI_ITIL_Operating_Model.pdf document hosted on SharePoint/VIB_Ops/Quality.
Escalation matrices must be documented in the Customer_Escalation_Tree.xlsx file hosted on SharePoint/VIB_Ops/Governance.
Tier-1 Hub Locations have a 4-hour on-site hardware replacement SLA subject to localized urban depot proximity.
Tier-2/3 Branch Locations have a Next Business Day (NBD) strict replacement target for edge SD-WAN/NGFW appliances.
The active VI Managed SOC ingests global Threat Intelligence feeds for automated containment.
The active VI Managed SOC triggers automated containment scripts via the SD-WAN controller against flagged internal IP addresses.
The SOC maintains unilateral authority to sever WAN routing to infected branch points during critical ransomware propagation.
The ITIL v4 Alignment document is referenced for operational governance.
The Customer_Escalation_Tree.xlsx document is referenced for escalation governance.
The VP, Managed Services Operations is the owner of governance for this process.
The NOC Director, SOC Director, and enterprise Customer Success are reviewers for this process.
Governance for this process is reviewed annually.
Day 2 operational support strictly ends at the LAN port of the VI-provided SD-WAN/Firewall router.
VI Business (India) is prohibited from troubleshooting internal customer LAN switching unless an IT OS-Management SOW is attached.
VI Business (India) is prohibited from troubleshooting local Wi-Fi unless an IT OS-Management SOW is attached.
VI Business (India) is prohibited from troubleshooting application-level server faults (e.g., SAP database hangs) unless an IT OS-Management SOW is attached.
An explicit IT OS-Management SOW is required for VI Business (India) to troubleshoot internal customer LAN switching, local Wi-Fi, or application-level server faults.
The framework outlines the corporate governance model, headcount allocations, and escalation hierarchy for VI Business (India).
Headcount numbers and organizational breakdowns must not be manipulated to artificially inflate proposals.
VI Business India operates as a structurally autonomous division (Enterprise Business Unit - EBU) within Vodafone Idea Limited (VIL).
The EBU's enterprise IT logistics and service delivery SLAs are shielded from consumer retail telecommunications divisions' operational fluctuations.
The Chief Enterprise Business Officer (CEBO) holds exclusive P&L authority and pinnacle escalation oversight for the EBU.
The Enterprise CISO oversees dedicated enterprise SOC operations and governs data privacy/DPDP Act compliance independently of network engineering variables.
The VP - Core Networks & Cloud manages the architectural lifecycle of SDN, Cloud Interconnects (ExpressRoute/Direct Connect), and the optical backbone.
The VP - Managed Services Operations governs NOC/SOC integration and controls 99.99% assurance SLAs.
The Head of EBU Delivery & PMO governs PRINCE2-aligned rollout squads.
Service Assurance (NOC/SOC) requires ~2,500 dedicated, fully certified (ITIL v4, CISSP, OEM Certs) L1/L2/L3 analysts.
Service Assurance (NOC/SOC) analysts operate on 24/7 tri-shift rotations.
Delivery & Field Engineering consists of ~3,500 deployment specialists organized across four geographic quadrants (North, West, South, East).
Product & Solutions Architecture includes ~1,000 deep-domain SMEs focusing on SD-WAN overlays and hyperscaler cloud topology.
Sales & Customer Success has ~3,000 dedicated account directors and relationship managers.
Enterprise rollouts (e.g., 300+ edge migrations) require matrixed execution.
The Executive Org Chart is documented in SharePoint/VIB_HR/EBU_Leadership.pptx and owned by VP HR - Enterprise.
The Delivery Sub-Chart is documented in SharePoint/VIB_Delivery/PMO_Matrix.vsdx and owned by the Head of PMO.
Governance of organizational structure is owned by VP, Human Resources (Enterprise).
Reviewers for organizational governance include the Enterprise CISO and VP Delivery.
Organizational governance reviews occur bi-annually.

Bid Teams must explicitly refuse providing the direct personal names of lower-level Tier 3 engineers or individual program managers during the primary RFP phase, committing only to verifiable industry certifications (e.g., PMP, CCIE) attached to those roles.
External third-party fiber splicing sub-contractors utilized for highly remote civil works are excluded from the direct 10,000+ employee count.
VI Business (India) is a Gold Certified Partner with Cisco/Meraki for scalable SD-WAN architecture requiring robust, application-aware routing overlays.
VI Business (India) is a Platinum/Expert level Partner with Fortinet for deploying NGFW and SD-WAN controller functions consolidated into a single branch appliance.
VI Business (India) utilizes Palo Alto Networks' Prisma SD-WAN and NGFW integrations for Zero-Trust enterprise perimeter requirements.

VI Business (India) is a Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner certified to install, manage, and monitor Layer 2/3 direct connections between Primary Data Centers and Azure’s Central India (Pune) region.
VI Business (India) is an AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner certified to terminate 10Gbps dedicated fibers directly into AWS ap-south-1 (Mumbai).

VI Business strictly utilizes a pre-tested, certified subset of OEM hardware for managed solutions. Proposing untested third-party OEMs is prohibited as it impacts the ability to guarantee the 99.99% network SLA.
The Managed SOC leverages Tier-1 commercial Threat Intelligence (including Recorded Future and CrowdStrike Intelligence).
Threat Intelligence feeds are integrated into DPDP Act-compliant Indian-hosted SIEM instances (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel or IBM QRadar).
The SIEM integration forms the baseline of a <4 hour P1 containment architecture.
Strategic alliances with primary M2M module manufacturers and GSMA-compliant remote-SIM provisioning (eSIM) platforms enable the native EMM structure to manage millions of active tags.
The Azure ExpressRoute Partner certification is verified via SharePoint/VIB_Alliances/Cert_Azure.pdf.
The AWS DX Delivery Partner certification is verified via SharePoint/VIB_Alliances/Cert_AWS.pdf.
The Cisco Gold Integrator certification is verified via SharePoint/VIB_Alliances/Cert_Cisco.pdf.
The Cloud Alliance Head owns the Azure ExpressRoute Partner certification.
The Cloud Alliance Head owns the AWS DX Delivery Partner certification.
The OEM Alliance Lead owns the Cisco Gold Integrator certification.
The VI Business (India) support SLA framework explicitly excludes unauthorized or gray-market hardware deployments.
Customers integrating legacy or uncertified CPEs into the managed overlay must sign a distinct Liability Waiver forfeiting stated SLAs.
The Pricing Catalog defines standard commercial models, OpEx frameworks, billing triggers, and volume thresholds for VI Business (India).
The document is applicable for designing Commercial Assumptions, OpEx matrices, and TCO evaluations in telecom bounds.
Indicative MRC for Edge Broadband (FTTx) is ₹ 8,000 - ₹ 15,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for Edge Broadband (FTTx) is ₹ 15,000.
Indicative MRC for DIA Enterprise Fiber is ₹ 25,000 - ₹ 75,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for DIA Enterprise Fiber is ₹ 50,000.
Indicative MRC for Active 4G/LTE Backup is ₹ 1,500 - ₹ 3,000 per month.
Indicative OTC for Active 4G/LTE Backup is ₹ 5,000 (including router).
Indicative OTC for Core Hub 1 Gbps PtP is ₹ 1,00,000.
Placeholder INR rates represent standard un-discounted rack rates; bid-specific discounting requires systematic Deal Desk margin validation based on deal TCV.
Structured network endpoints (CPE/Firewalls) remain the capital property of VI Business unless an explicit asset transfer clause is authorized.
SD-WAN and Edge Connectivity Brackets costing includes underlay bandwidth, overlay orchestrated routing, CPE hardware leasing, and unified NBD break-fix support.
DIA Enterprise Fiber offers bandwidth capacities ranging from 50 Mbps to 500 Mbps.
DIA Enterprise Fiber pricing ranges from ₹ 25,000 to ₹ 75,000 per month.
DIA Enterprise Fiber includes an active 4G/LTE backup as part of the service.
DIA Enterprise Fiber provides a pooled 50GB data allocation.
DIA Enterprise Fiber charges between ₹ 1,500 and ₹ 3,000 for additional services.
DIA Enterprise Fiber includes a router with an indicative one-time charge of ₹ 5,000.
Core Hub provides 1 Gbps point-to-point (PtP) connectivity.
Core Hub supports full duplex non-linear dimensioning (NLD).
Core Hub has an indicative one-time charge (OTC) of ₹ 1,00,000.
Connectivity Tier pricing includes an indicative one-time charge (OTC) of ₹ 1,50,000+ (varies by distance).
Hyperscaler Interconnect Subscriptions have bandwidth capacities with indicative monthly recurring charges (MRC) and one-time charges (OTC).
Managed SOC and SIEM Licensing pricing scales based on ingestion volume (Events Per Second or daily log GB) and integrated SD-WAN edge points.
Managed SOC and SIEM Licensing prohibits surprise hourly 'time-and-materials' charges for P1 anomalies.
IoT Enterprise Mobility (EMM) offers volume discounts at scale markers: 100k, 500k, 1M, and 2.5M SIMs.
IoT Enterprise Mobility (EMM) uses aggregate Custom APN data pools to prevent individual endpoint overage penalties unless organizational limits are breached.
IoT Enterprise Mobility (EMM) prohibits individual endpoint overage penalties unless total organizational data limits are exceeded.
Pricing is illustrative and subject to regional transmission variants.
Monthly billing for an individual location is securely activated 24 hours after validated ZTP configuration loading and formal User Acceptance Sign-off on stability.
The Master Rate Card (SharePoint/VIB_Commercial/Master_RateCard.xlsx) is owned by the Head of Pricing.
CPE Transport Costs data is maintained in the Logistics_Depreciation_Engine.xlsx owned by Supply Chain.
Connectivity Tier pricing is illustrative and subject to regional transmission variants; indicative MRC and OTC values are provided as baseline representations.

Legacy telecommunications contract termination penalties owed by the client to an incumbent carrier for disconnecting legacy MPLS are excluded and will not be absorbed or 'bought out' by VI Business (India).
VI Business (India) must codify its stance on data residency, network telemetry storage, and localized compliance to align with India’s DPDP Act 2023.
The document serves as evidence of alignment with India’s DPDP Act 2023 for answering RFP questions on data residency, SIEM log retention, and cross-border transfer limitations.
The enterprise Customer is the Data Fiduciary, and VI Business (India) acts as the Data Processor, except for direct employee billing parameters where VI may act as a Fiduciary.

100% data localization is enforced for core enterprise networking and security operations, with all SD-WAN orchestrator backups, configuration templates, and SOC SIEM logs stored exclusively in India.
source_section: 3.1 DPDP Act 2023 Conformity and Data Residency

policy_text: Log data and payload traffic on managed SD-WAN backbones or ExpressRoute fabrics must not route outside Indian jurisdiction without explicit, documented BGP configuration requests from the Customer.
source_section: 3.1 DPDP Act 2023 Conformity and Data Residency

policy_text: SIEM logs must adhere to a 90-day 'Hot Storage' retention period and a 12-month 'Cold Archive' rotation unless secondary regulatory mandates require longer retention.
source_section: 3.2 Managed SOC and SIEM Privacy Controls

policy_text: Access to historical SIEM logs is restricted to vetted SOC analysts via Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) during active incident investigations.
source_section: 3.2 Managed SOC and SIEM Privacy Controls

policy_text: The Enterprise Mobility Management (EMM) platform collects location metadata and ICCID connectivity traces from over 2.5 million IoT SIMs.
Telemetry is processed exclusively for localized billing optimization, coverage mapping, and SIM lifecycle management.
Identity linkages connecting SIMs to individual operational personnel are tokenized to mathematically satisfy Privacy by Design protocols.
VI Business operates as a Unified License (UL) holder under the Department of Telecommunications (DoT), balancing enterprise privacy expectations with national security mandates.
source_section: Lawful Interception (Regulatory Monitoring) As a Unified License (UL) holder

policy_text: Customer commercial payload data remains encrypted in transit via SD-WAN (IPsec/TLS boundary), but underlying network telemetry streams are subject to interception by authorized Indian intelligence agencies without Customer notification.
source_section: Lawful Interception (Regulatory Monitoring) As a Unified License (UL) holder

policy_text: Architecture Privacy Impact documentation is stored in SharePoint/VIB_Privacy as evidence for privacy topic assessments.
Localization Attestation documentation is stored in SharePoint/VIB_Legal as evidence for data localization compliance.
VI Business exercises no control or processing rights over Data Subjects interacting directly with Customer-owned application layer databases (e.g., Customer CRMs hosted in AWS).
The Master Catalog is the exhaustive, definitive dictionary outlining all universally available VI Business (India) solutions and associated SKU terminology.
Adherence to the Master Catalog taxonomy is required to ensure proposed solutions map flawlessly to actual operational fulfillment systems.
The Master Catalog must be verbatim inserted into RFP 'Product Compliance Checklists', 'Proposed Solution Inventories', and Bills of Materials (BOM).
SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) is defined as the unique identifier for VI fulfillment.
CPE (Customer Premises Equipment) is defined as equipment located at the customer's site.
Missing items from the Master Catalog signify non-standard capabilities.
Submission of non-catalog solutions requires explicit, prolonged Deal Desk/Engineering approvals.
Enterprise Broadband (VI-ACC-FBR-DIA) provides Dedicated Internet Access with speeds of 50 Mbps up to 1 Gbps, symmetrical SLA-backed fiber delivery.
Cellular Backup (VI-ACC-LTE-B2B) offers managed 4G/LTE active backup via industrial SIM with failover preservation.
Core Transmission (VI-ACC-NLD-PTP) delivers Point-to-Point National Long Distance optical paths connecting primary Tier-1 Data Centers.
SD-WAN Standard Edge provides hardware-agnostic basic overlay enabling Application-Aware routing.
SD-WAN Standard Edge supports Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP).
SD-WAN Standard Edge is hardware-agnostic.
SD-WAN Advanced Edge includes a converged SD-WAN controller with embedded Next-Gen Firewall (NGFW) for deep packet inspection.
Hub Aggregation (VI-SDW-HA-HUB) uses dual-chassis High Availability (Active/Active) nodes.
Hub Aggregation (VI-SDW-HA-HUB) is designed exclusively to handle 99.99% uptime dependencies.
Azure Cross-Connect provides a dedicated ExpressRoute pipe to Azure Central India (Pune) MMR from NESCO DC with <8ms latency.
Azure Cross-Connect latency must be less than 8ms.
AWS Cross-Connect (VI-CLD-AWS-DX) provides Layer 2/3 Direct Connect termination into AWS ap-south-1 (Mumbai).
Hub Aggregation (VI-SDW-HA-HUB) uses dual-chassis High Availability (Active/Active) nodes.
SD-WAN Advanced Edge embeds a Next-Gen Firewall (NGFW) for deep packet inspection.
AWS Cross-Connect (VI-CLD-AWS-DX) terminates Layer 2/3 Direct Connect directly into AWS ap-south-1 (Mumbai).
DPDP Act localized log ingestion must be 100% India-hosted.
SIEM ingestion must scale per EPS/GB bounds.
Managed Threat Response must provide 24/7/365 active boundary containment using ITIL v4 methodologies.
EMM Platform Portal must support dynamic scaling across millions of active tags.
Secure Endpoint SIM must be locked to an isolated VI Custom APN (cAPN) to prevent public routing.
Internal spec sheets and product decks must be stored in SharePoint/VIB_Products directory.
VP, Product Management is designated as the owner of the product portfolio.
Reviewers for policy decisions include VP Core Networks, Deal Desk, and Enterprise CISO.
Quarterly alignment with OEM firmware cycles and catalog depreciation rules is required for policy reviews.
High-bandwidth Private Cloud Interconnects (VI-CLD-AZURE-ER scaling over 10 Gbps) require localized capacity validation checks against Mumbai NESCO optical transport nodes before quoting.
Reselling of bare-metal AWS/Azure compute VMs (non-networking) is excluded from VI Business core networking solutions and requires separate systems integration partnerships.
The Project Governance Framework defines how VI Business (India) structures authority, escalation lines, and risk mitigation across complex, high-risk telecommunications modernization programs.
The framework provides enterprise evaluation committees with proof of operational maturity.
The framework is essential for accurately populating RFP segments addressing 'Project Management Planning,' 'Issue Escalations,' 'Steering Committees,' and 'Risk Matrices.'
Deployments must adhere to a cascading three-tier governance hierarchy ensuring unblocked velocities.
The Operational Tier (Weekly cadence) is governed by a VI Project Manager certified in PRINCE2/PMP.
The Operational Tier focuses on field mobilization velocity, logistical tracking of Edge CPE hardware, and daily updates to the active RAID log.
The Management Tier (Monthly cadence) is governed by the VI Delivery Director and evaluates macro progress against the baseline master schedule.
The Management Tier re-engineers blocked pathways such as severe Right of Way constraints or civil delays affecting datacenter trunks.
The Steering Committee (Quarterly cadence) is governed by Enterprise Board/VP-level Sponsors and authorizes financial viability of mass migration phase gateways.
The Steering Committee verifies the integrity of long-range DPDP compliance metrics.
The Pilot Phase Gateway requires successful testing of SIEM data log ingestion before transitioning to Phase 2/3 mass-rollout.
The SteerCo rejects transition from Pilot Phase until SOC tracking is fully verified off pilot branches.

The ZTP Deployment Gateway requires explicit functional sign-off indicating Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) cloud orchestrator transmits and locks exact Low-Level Design (LLD) states onto deployed hardware without manual overrides.
VI Business utilizes rigid QA release checkpoints mathematically mapped to delivery milestones.
The RACI Matrix (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed) must be used to define roles and responsibilities in governance processes.
The RAID Log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies) must be maintained for tracking project elements.
Structural deviations from the finalized solution design (LLD) require a formal Change Request (CR) workflow to recalibrate delivery SLAs and commercial TCV.
VI Business is Responsible and Accountable for the execution of the overlay implementation and hardware delivery.
The Enterprise Customer is Responsible for unblocking physical branch access, extending local LAN bridging, and establishing primary business contact authorization.
The Baseline RAID Tracker must be stored at SharePoint/VIB_Delivery/Templates/RAID_v2_1.
The RACI Distribution Matrix must be stored at SharePoint/VIB_Delivery/Templates/RACI_NetTransform.
The Baseline RAID Tracker is owned by the Enterprise PMO.
The RACI Distribution Matrix is owned by the Head of Solutions.
SteerCo executives (VP/SVP level) must be formally nominated following contract execution and MSA mobilization.
Exact functional titles (rather than personal names) must be legally supplied during initial RFP responses to negate structural re-organizations.
Timelines formally suspend (clock-stops) if the Client fails to approve the fundamental PID and High-Level Design (HLD) constructs.
The Proposal Content Library is for Tier-1 RFP responses and contains pre-approved narrative language, not objective technical facts.
For strict SLA guarantees and liability structures, primary KB artifacts must be consulted, not the Proposal Content Library.
Bid teams must append exact client terminology to boilerplates to finalize connections.
Tier-1 Hub boilerplates must not be used for standard, non-SLA broadband connectivity structures.
The Network Transformation Programme converges Active-Active SD-WAN fabrics, direct private Hyperscaler pipes, and a localized ITIL v4 Managed Security (SOC) wrap.
The Phased Migration Framework uses Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) and pre-profiled CPE hardware for rapid deployment scaling.
The Managed SOC operates from Tier-III facilities in India and complies with DPDP Act data residency mandates.
source_section: 3.3 Positioning the Managed SOC and SLAs

policy_text: Priority 1 anomalies must be identified within 15 minutes and network isolation protocols executed within 4 hours.
source_section: 3.3 Positioning the Managed SOC and SLAs

policy_text: VI Business is certified as an official Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner and AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner.
Cloud interconnects enforce latency geometries consistently beneath 8ms to primary hyperscaler edges.
Enterprise IoT scale is managed via Custom APN architectural security and Enterprise Mobility Management (EMM) integration.
Endpoint traffic from IoT devices must be restricted entirely away from the public internet.
Corporate Framing is governed by Corporate Profile [VIB-CORP-002] with the Head of Bid Management as the Governance Owner.
Security Language is governed by Security Overview Pack [VIB-SEC-001] with the Enterprise CISO as the Governance Owner.
Do not inject the precise values defined in these boilerplates into the formal "RFP Compliance/SLA Tables" matrix without consulting the SLA and KPI Standard Matrix [VIB-COM-005].
The Reference Architecture Library maintains pre-certified network and security topologies for VI Business (India).
Deployments must adhere to the documented architectural bounds to structurally guarantee SLA compliance during Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) escalations.
Solution Architects, Pre-Sales Engineers, and Technical RFP Responders are the intended audience for this document.
Custom architectural shapes beyond defined templates require manual review by the Principal Enterprise Architect.
Unvetted architectures void standard SLA penalty guarantees.
Tier-1 Hub Architecture must use High-Availability (HA) clustered chassis edge nodes.
Tier-1 Hub Architecture underlay fabric must include Primary 100+ Mbps Dedicated Internet Access (DIA) Fibre and Secondary isolated 4G/5G LTE macro.
Tier-1 Hub Architecture must use BGP for dynamic path propagation and ECMP for Active/Active transport.
Tier-1 Hub Architecture must execute stateful sub-50ms failover for latency-intolerant payloads without destroying application handshake sessions.
Tier-2/3 Branch Architecture must use a single discrete SD-WAN Edge/Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) appliance per location.
Tier-1 Active-Active HA architecture source files are stored in SharePoint/VIB_Arch/Tier1_Dual_CPE.vsdx.
NESCO to Azure ExpressRoute architecture source files are stored in SharePoint/VIB_Arch/Azure_ER_Logical.vsdx.
Tier-1 Hub Architecture must achieve 99.99% availability.
Dedicated dual 10 Gbps Layer 2/3 circuits must originate directly from VI’s NESCO IT Park (Mumbai) or Chennai Tier-III nodes.
source_section: 3.3 Data Center to Hyperscaler Interconnect Architecture

policy_text: Azure ExpressRoute must implement Active-Active resilient eBGP, anchoring in Azure Central India (Pune) to support dedicated Microsoft and Private Peerings.
source_section: 3.3 Data Center to Hyperscaler Interconnect Architecture

policy_text: AWS Direct Connect must integrate specialized pipelines terminating into high-availability AWS Transit Gateways mapped to the ap-south-1 region.
source_section: 3.3 Data Center to Hyperscaler Interconnect Architecture

policy_text: Sub-8ms round trip base-latency must be maintained independent of public ISPs.
source_section: 3.3 Data Center to Hyperscaler Interconnect Architecture

policy_text: Fleet sensors must communicate across VI’s nationwide macro RAN base (250,000+ cell sites) terminating in dynamically isolated Packet Data Network Gateways (PGW).
Traffic flows must exclusively use heavily localized Custom APNs (cAPN), with IoT payload permanently encrypted and separated from the wider internet grid.
Custom APNs (cAPN) must directly intersect the corporate network backbone via localized private IP addressing.
Tier-1 Active-Active HA architecture must be documented in the SharePoint/VIB_Arch/Tier1_Dual_CPE.vsdx Visio artifact.
NESCO to Azure ExpressRoute architecture must be documented in the SharePoint/VIB_Arch/Azure_ER_Logical.vsdx Visio artifact.
Principal Enterprise Architect must be the owner of the architecture governance.
VP Core Networks, Head of Managed Services, and Enterprise CISO must review the architecture.
Architecture reviews must be updated concurrently with major OEM firmware capability releases or yearly network infrastructure refreshes.
Designing a 99.99% Tier-1 SLA topology requires the customer to provide dual, distinct power phases backed by independent UPS units on-site.
All architectures must utilize exclusively Indian-operated management planes to comply with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023.

Internal Control assertions regarding DPDP Act alignment, telemetry routing, and <4 hour response targets are binding legal commitments and must not be altered within the RFP narrative without formal security escalation.
All assertions surrounding DPDP Act alignment must be maintained as binding legal commitments.
The Managed SOC operates from Tier-III certified facilities in Pune, Maharashtra.
P1 Threat (Exploitation/Active Breach) must be acknowledged within 15 minutes.
P1 Threat containment (network isolation) must be initiated within <4 hours.
P2 Event (Elevated Risk/DDoS Attempt) must be acknowledged within 30 minutes.
P2 Event resolution tracking must occur within <8 hours.
Incident, Problem, and Change Management processes must follow ITIL v4 Framework standards.
NGFW capabilities must include granular URL filtering (60+ active categorical defenses), DPI, and stateful IPS engines.
NGFW throughput per appliance must range dynamically between 200 Mbps to 1 Gbps.
NGFW latency profiles must not degrade below <50ms failover paths.
SIEM and Threat Intelligence Log Correlation must be implemented as a technical capability.
source_section: 3.3 SIEM and Threat Intelligence Log Correlation

policy_text: Standard multitenant cloud SIEM architectures accept upward of 5000+ Events Per Second (EPS) without requiring bespoke hardware node expansion.
Core network event logs and SIEM syslog traces maintain a 90-day 'Hot' retention schedule and a standard 12-month archival rotation, isolated completely within Indian jurisdiction.
Active commercial feed aggregation (CrowdStrike, Recorded Future) provides immediate SIEM IOC correlation matching across the domestic backbone.
VI Business enforces absolute regulatory supremacy concerning internal data handling routines under DPDP Act 2023 and Indian Data Sovereignty.
100% of SIEM log data, threat intelligence aggregations, and identity token handshakes remain natively stored in Indian Tier-III datacenters.
Unauthorized cross-border routing of telemetry analytics data is prohibited without explicit consent of the Customer (Data Fiduciary).
DPDP Alignment Policy is stored in SharePoint/VIB_Compliance_Vault/Data_Locality.pdf.
SOC Infrastructure Design is documented in SharePoint/VIB_Security/SOC_Architecture_HLD.vsdx.
The Data Privacy Officer owns the DPDP Alignment Policy.
The Lead SOC Architect owns the SOC Infrastructure Design.
Containment rigorously entails blocking hostile traffic from laterally progressing across the managed Wide Area Network.

VI Business (India) does not assume responsibility for sanitizing malware directly off customer-owned server clusters or manipulating standalone end-user compute devices unless an explicit Endpoint Detection (EDR) commercial scope is integrated.
VI Business operates dedicated hyperscaler Meet-Me-Rooms (MMR) anchored primarily within NESCO IT Park, Mumbai DC, and Chennai DC to bypass standard ISP latency variables.

policy_text: The owner of the policy is the VP, Core Networks.

policy_text: The reviewers of the policy are the VP Engineering and Enterprise CISO.

policy_text: The policy must be reviewed bi-annually.

policy_text: Hardware replacement in remote Tier-3 locations is constrained by proximity to designated regional engineering depots mapped during the site survey phase.
source_filename: Service Coverage and Infrastructure Footprint Document.pdf

policy_text: Global cross-border international links (e.g., Mumbai to London IPLC) are excluded from this standard domestic policy and operate under distinct contractual rules and international carrier SLAs.
source_filename: Service Coverage and Infrastructure Footprint Document.pdf

policy_text: The SLA and KPI Standard Matrix provides an authoritative schedule of service level commitments, including uptime, latency, MTTR, and incident reaction boundaries.
The SLA and KPI Standard Matrix serves as the absolute ceiling for operational guarantees made during competitive bidding.
The intended audience for this document is Service Managers, Commercial Analysts, and RFP Writers.
The document is applicable for responding securely to RFP Service Level Annexures with quantitative targets backed by financial penalty frameworks.
MTTR is defined as Mean Time to Repair / Resolve.
P1/P2/P3/P4 priority incident levels map to ITIL v4 incident models.
Promising any SLA target superior to those defined in Section 3.1 requires authorization from the VP Managed Services Operations and Regional CFO.
Tier-1 Hub (Dual Link Active/Active) has a 99.99% monthly SLA target.
Tier-1 Hub SLA measurement requires redundant power / HA Chassis.
Tier-2/3 Branch (Active/Standby ZTP) has a 99.95% monthly SLA target.
Tier-2/3 Branch SLA is measured at the SD-WAN CPE edge.
SD-WAN Link Failover Event must execute stateful session preservation with a threshold of <50ms per event.
ExpressRoute Latency (NESCO to Azure) must be <8ms round trip, based on 95th percentile polling.
Direct Connect Port Availability must be 99.9% monthly.
P1 (Critical) incidents (core systemic failure, active network breach, ransomware pivot) require 15-minute acknowledgment and containment within 4 hours.
source_section: 3.2 SOC Incident Response Targets (ITIL v4 Aligned)

policy_text: P2 (High) incidents (degradation of critical HA link, DDoS attempt, elevated SIEM alert) require 30-minute acknowledgment and resolution within 8 hours.
source_section: 3.2 SOC Incident Response Targets (ITIL v4 Aligned)

policy_text: P3 (Medium) incidents (intermittent jitter/latency, localized performance issues) require 2-hour acknowledgment and resolution within 24 hours.
source_section: 3.2 SOC Incident Response Targets (ITIL v4 Aligned)

policy_text: P4 (Low) incidents (standard service requests, policy changes, routine queries) require 4-hour acknowledgment and resolution within 3 days.
source_section: 3.2 SOC Incident Response Targets (ITIL v4 Aligned)

policy_text: P1 (Critical) incident updates must be provided every 1 hour.
P2 (High) incident updates must be provided every 2 hours.
P3 (Medium) incident updates must be provided every 8 hours.
P4 (Low) incident updates must be provided daily.
The company must define and maintain a Metric Target for service performance.
An Enforcement Engine must be implemented to monitor and enforce service compliance.
Service reports must be generated in the specified formats, including Monthly Service PDF.
Network Availability must be monitored and reported through the Overlay Orchestrator Dashboard.
SOC Containment limits must be enforced to ensure security compliance.
ITSM ServiceNow Log Engine must be utilized for logging service incidents.
SLA Exception Reports must be generated to document service deviations.
SLA financial credits are capped at 10% to 15% of the offending service's MRC, aligning with liability structures in Standard Terms and Conditions.
Service interruptions caused by mass regional power grid collapses, global hyperscaler outages, or customer-side infrastructure vandalism automatically suspend MTTR measurements.
The Standard Commercial Policy establishes financial guardrails, hardware leasing mechanisms, discount threshold authorities, and contract structuring mechanics for managed enterprise networks.

The policy applies to Deal Desk, Finance Managers, Sales Leads, and Bid Directors for drafting RFP Commercial Assumptions, generating OpEx TCO evaluations, and detailing minimum contract duration lock-ins.
Deviations from standard OpEx modeling require Vice President level corporate finance authorization when selling core hardware (CapEx) instead of leasing it.
VI Business (India) must standardize on an end-to-end 'as-a-Service' connectivity model for SD-WAN Edge CPE, cellular failover links, and core SIEM ingestion licenses.
VI retains full capital ownership of all provided hardware throughout the MSA term.
Monthly billing commences strictly 24 hours after validation of a site's Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) profile load and formal User Acceptance Testing (UAT).
For multi-tiered rollups, billing scales linearly as individual branch site batches go live, with sites lacking active WAN delivery remaining dormant and unbilled.

For long-term MSAs extending beyond 36 months, extreme inflationary fluctuations exceeding standard CPI limits require mandatory commercial indexing protection clauses or re-negotiation gates defined heavily within the master agreement.
The enterprise customer explicitly bears direct financial liability for theft, vandalism, or gross negligence resulting in physical destruction of edge CPE units positioned inside their premises.

Massive IoT fleet distributions utilize aggregate Custom APN data pools to shield customers from erratic billing variations. Individual endpoint overages do not trigger penalty rates unless the total accumulated organizational limit reaches 100% capacity.

Network ports terminating at Azure (ExpressRoute) and AWS (Direct Connect) are priced strictly upon the committed bandwidth pipe. The VI Business MRC does not absorb egress data transfer taxes levied individually by the cloud provider against the customer.
Accumulated monthly SLA financial credits crossing all tracked KPI parameters must never exceed 10% to 15% of that specific, offending link/service’s individual MRC.

Sustained non-payment of uncontested invoices exceeding 60-90 days past the due date structurally empowers VI Business (India) to initiate a staged suspension of non-critical data flows, escalating to hard port isolation, prior to formal contract termination.
Deal Authorization DOAs are tracked in SharePoint/VIB_Finance/Delegation_Of_Authority.pdf and owned by Corporate Finance.
Hardware Break-Fix Fees are tracked in SharePoint/VIB_Finance/Depreciation_Engine.xlsx and owned by Supply Chain Finance.
Legacy third-party telecom termination fees will not be inherently 'bought out' or subtracted from VI Business (India)'s OpEx proposals without exceptional TCV escalations.
VI Business (India) must enforce rigid contractual positions to isolate liability and maintain DPDP Act compliance in all enterprise telecommunication and cybersecurity services.
The Master Services Agreement (MSA) must be appended to RFP responses when clients demand an advance copy of base commercial terms or for answering 'Legal Compliance' annexures.
Accepting a Customer's MSA draft without injecting standard protective terms requires VP Corporate Finance and General Counsel authorization.
VI Business (India)'s total aggregate liability is strictly capped at 100% of the total fees paid by the Customer for the specific affected Service during the 12 months preceding the claim event.
Neither party may be liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, special, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, revenue, or data loss.
The sole and exclusive remedy for SLA metric failures is the issuance of Service Credits.
source_section: 3.2 Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalties / Liquidated Damages

policy_text: Accumulated Service Credits may not exceed 10% of the Monthly Recurring Charge (MRC) for the affected site or service in the month of failure.
source_section: 3.2 Service Level Agreement (SLA) Penalties / Liquidated Damages

policy_text: VI Business (India) must comply with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, for data privacy and sovereignty.
The Customer acknowledges they act as the 'Data Fiduciary' and VI Business (India) acts solely as the 'Data Processor' regarding payload data traversing the managed networks.

All SIEM log data generated by the Managed SOC and network telemetry for assurance must be processed and stored exclusively within the sovereign territory of India, aligned with the DPDP Act 2023. No cross-border data transfer of management telemetry is permitted.

Services are provisioned under the Unified License (UL) granted by the Department of Telecommunications (DoT), India. VI Business must comply with all lawful interception, monitoring, and decryption directives from authorized Indian intelligence and security agencies without prior notification to the Customer.

Managed Services are subject to a minimum lock-in period of 36 months. Termination for convenience during this period requires the Customer to pay the remaining MRC for the entire unexpired lock-in duration plus unrecovered capital costs.
The Baseline MSA Text is governed by the General Counsel, and the DoT Compliance Annex is governed by the VP, Regulatory Affairs.
Exceptions to the Liability Cap or SLA Penalty Caps require escalation to the Deal Desk as documented in the Deviation or Fallback Legal Positions Guide [VIB-LEGAL-002].
SLA measurement methodologies mapping to commercial penalties are defined in the SLA and KPI Standard Matrix [VIB-COM-005].
